title: Object detection
abstract: A system for detecting the presence of an object in a doorway having a powered door is disclosed, the system comprising: a radiation transmitter (202) adapted to direct radiation substantially along a longitudinal edge of the doorway in a direction substantially perpendicular to a direction of motion of said powered door; a radiation receiver (204) for receiving the radiation transmitted by the radiation transmitter; a processor configured to detect interruption of said radiation by an object whereby to detect the presence of an object in the doorway.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A system for detecting the presence of an object in a doorway having a powered door, the system comprising: a radiation transmitter adapted to direct radiation substantially along a longitudinal edge of the doorway in a direction substantially perpendicular to a direction of motion of said powered door; a radiation receiver for receiving the radiation transmitted by the radiation transmitter; a processor configured to detect interruption of said radiation by an object whereby to detect the presence of an object in the doorway. 2. A system according to claim 1 wherein the transmitter is adapted to direct radiation towards a reflective element at the opposing end of the doorway, the receiver being adapted to detect the radiation reflected from said reflective element. 3. A system according to claim 2 wherein the reflective element is a sill of the doorway. 4. A system according to any preceding claim wherein the radiation transmitter and radiation receiver are adapted to be affixed to the same side of the doorway. 5. A system according to claim 2 or 3 wherein the radiation transmitter is adapted to be affixed at one side of the doorway and the radiation receiver is adapted to be affixed at the opposing side of the doorway. 6. A system according to any preceding claim wherein the radiation transmitter comprises a collimated light source. 7. A system according to claim 6 wherein the collimated light source comprises a laser. 8. A system according to claim 7 wherein the laser comprises a laser diode. 9. A system according to claim 6 wherein the collimated light source comprises a collimated LED. 10. A system according to any preceding claim comprising a modulator to modulate the transmitted radiation. 1 1. A system according to claim 10 wherein the modulation comprises pulsing the transmitted radiation. 12. A system according to any preceding claim wherein the radiation transmitter is adapted to transmit light in the visible spectrum. 13. A system according to any preceding claim further comprising means for limiting the operation of the system. 14. A system according to claim 13 wherein the means for limiting the operation of the system is operable to limit the operation of the system in dependence on the separation of the doorway. 15. A system according to claim 14 wherein the means for limiting the operation of the system limits the operation of the system based on a signal for the doorway to close. 16. A system according to any preceding claim further comprising a separation sensor for detecting the separation of the doorway. 17. A system according claim 16 wherein the separation sensor is adapted to detect when the separation of the doorway is less than a predetermined separation. 18. A system according to any preceding claim wherein the processor is adapted to enable the system when the powered doors are closing and disable the system when the powered doors are opening. 19. A system according to any preceding claim wherein the processor is adapted to enable the system when the powered doors are within a predetermined separation, the predetermined separation preferably being 100mm, preferably 20mm. 20. A system according to any preceding claim wherein the radiation receiver is adapted to be integrated into a door safety edge device. 21. A system according to claim 20 wherein the radiation receiver is integrated into a door safety edge device end cap for said integration into the door safety edge device door safety edge device. 22. A system according to any preceding claim wherein the processor is adapted to control the movement of the powered door on detection of said interruption. 23. A system according to any preceding claim wherein the processor is adapted to discriminate between a plurality of different types of said received radiation (e.g. between background radiation and radiation received directly or indirectly from said transmitter). 24. A system according to claim 23 wherein the radiation transmitter is adapted to modulate the transmitted radiation. 25. A system according to claim 24 wherein the modulation comprises pulsing the transmitted radiation. 26. A system according to claim 24 or 25 wherein discriminating between a plurality of different types of said received radiation comprises detecting the modulation of the radiation. 27. A system according to any of claims 23 to 26 wherein discriminating between a plurality of different types of said received radiation comprises detecting a spectral property of the radiation. 28. A system according to claim 27 wherein the spectral property is a wavelength band. 29. A system according to claim 26 or 27 wherein the spectral property is an intensity. 30. A system according to any preceding claim wherein the transmitter is adapted to be integrated into a door edge and the system further comprises a radiation redirecting means adapted to be affixed to the door edge so as to redirect the transmitted radiation 

 substantially along a longitudinal edge of the doorway in a direction substantially perpendicular to a direction of motion of said powered door. 31. A system according to claim 30 wherein the radiation redirecting means is a mirror or prism. 32. A system according to claim 30 or 31 wherein the radiation redirecting means is sprung so as to protrude from the door edge. 33. An edge device for a powered door, the edge device comprising a radiation transmitter for the system of any of claims 1 to 32: the radiation transmitter being adapted to direct radiation substantially along the longitudinal edge of the doorway in the direction substantially perpendicular to the direction of motion of said powered door. 34. An edge device according to claim 33 wherein the radiation transmitter is transversely offset from the edge device. 35. An edge device according to claim 33 or 34 wherein the transmitter is located towards a longitudinal end of said edge device. 36. An edge device according to any of claims 33 to 35 further comprising an array of radiation transmitters adapted to transmit radiation substantially perpendicular to the longitudinal edge of the doorway. 37. An edge device according to any of claims 33 to 35 further comprising an array of radiation receivers adapted to receive radiation from a direction substantially perpendicular to the longitudinal edge of the doorway. 38. An edge device according to any of claims 33 to 37 further comprising a radiation receiver at the opposing end of the edge device to the radiation transmitter. 39. An edge device according to claim 38 wherein the radiation receiver is positioned so as to face substantially away from the radiation transmitter. 40. An edge device for powered door, the edge device comprising a radiation receiver for the system of any of claims 1 to 32: the radiation receiver being located towards a longitudinal end of said edge device and being adapted to receive the radiation transmitted by the radiation transmitter. 41. An edge device according to claim 40 further comprising an array of further radiation receivers adapted to receive radiation from a direction substantially perpendicular to the longitudinal edge of the edge device. 42. An edge device according to claim 40 further comprising an array of radiation transmitters adapted to transmit radiation substantially perpendicular to the longitudinal edge of the edge device. 43. An edge device according to any of claims 33 to 42 wherein the edge device is adapted to be fitted to a longitudinal edge of a powered door. 44. A controller for the system of any of claims 1 to 32 comprising said processor for detection of interruption of transmitted radiation. 45. A method of detecting the presence of an object in a doorway, the method comprising the steps of: transmitting, at a transmitter, radiation substantially along the longitudinal edge of the doorway; receiving radiation at a receiver; and controlling the movement of said doorway in dependence on said received radiation. 46. A system for detecting the presence of an object in a doorway of an elevator having a powered door, the system comprising: a radiation source for transmitting at least two radiation beams; two or more radiation receivers for receiving the radiation beams transmitted by the radiation source; and a processor configured to detect sequential interruption of radiation received by a first and a second radiation receiver; wherein the processor is adapted to detect the presence of an object in the doorway when said sequential interruption of radiation received by said first and second radiation receivers is detected. 47. A system according to claim 46 wherein the processor is configured to trigger a safety response when an object is detected. 48. A system according to claim 47, wherein the safety response is to return the elevator to the previous floor and open the powered doorway. 49. A system according to of claims 46 to 48, wherein the processor is further configured to determine the direction of motion of the elevator. 50. A system according to claim 49, wherein the processor is configured to determine the direction of motion of the elevator based on information relating to the elevator's current position and destination. 51. A system according to claim 49, further comprising an accelerometer, wherein the processor is configured to determine the direction of motion of the elevator based on an acceleration detected by the accelerometer. 52. A system according to any of claims claim 49 to 51 wherein an object is detected if the sequential interruption of the radiation beams is detected is consistent with the determined direction of motion of the elevator. 53. A system according to any preceding claim, further comprising a separation sensor for detecting the separation of the doorway; preferably wherein the separation sensor is adapted to detect when the separation of the doorway is less than a predetermined separation. 54. A system according to claim 53, wherein the processor is adapted to enable the system when the powered doors are within a predetermined separation. 55. A system according to claims 53 or 54, wherein the predetermined distance is less than 20mm, and preferably when the doors are substantially closed. 56. A system according to any of claims 53 to 55, wherein the separation sensor comprises at least one of: a Hall Effect sensor; and said radiation transmitter and said radiation receivers. 57. A system according to any of claims 46 to 56, wherein the radiation source and radiation receiver array are affixed to opposite sides of the doorway. 58. A system according to any of claims 46 to 56, wherein the radiation source and radiation receiver array are affixed to the same side of the doorway. 59. A system according to any of claims 46 to 58, wherein the radiation receivers are spaced along an edge of the powered doorway. 60. A system according to any of claims 46 to 59, wherein the first and second radiation receivers are neighbouring in the receiver array. 61. A system according to any of claims 46 to 60 wherein the radiation source is adapted to modulate the transmitted radiation. 62. A controller for the system of any of claims 46 to 61 , the controller comprising: a processor configured to detect when radiation received by two or more of the radiation receivers is interrupted, and to control the movement of the powered doorway in dependence on the order in which the interruptions are detected. 63. A controller according to claim 62 wherein the processor is further configured to control the movement of the elevator in dependence on the sequence in which the interruptions are detected. 64. A controller device according to claims 62 or 63 being operable to detect when the separation of the powered doorway is below a pre-determined distance, and controlling the movement of powered doorway in dependence on this separation. 65. An edge device comprising two or more radiation receivers, the edge device being suitable for providing a signal to the controller of any of claims 62 to 64. 66. An edge device according to claim 65 comprising means for signalling the interruption of each of said radiation receivers independently. 67. An edge device according to claim 65 or 66 comprising a separation sensor for detecting the separation of the doorway; preferably wherein the separation sensor is adapted to detect when the separation of the doorway is less than a predetermined separation. 68. A lift door incorporating an edge device of any of claims 33 to 43 or 65 to 67. 69. A lift system incorporating a lift door according to claim 68. 70. A method of detecting the presence of an object in a doorway of an elevator having a powered door, the method comprising steps of: receiving radiation at two or more radiation receivers; and determining sequential interruption of radiation received at a first and a second radiation receiver; thereby detecting the presence of an object in the doorway. 71. A method according to claim 70 comprising triggering a safety response when an object is detected. 72. A system according to claim 71 , wherein the safety response is to return the elevator to the previous floor and open the powered doorway. 73. A method according to any of claims 70 to 72, comprising determining the direction of motion off the elevator. 74. A method according to claim 73, comprising determining the direction of motion of the elevator based on information relating to the current position and destination of the elevator. 75. A method according to claims 73 or 74 wherein the presence of an object is detected if the sequence of interruption of the radiation beams is consistent with the determined direction of motion of the elevator. 76. A method according to any of claims 70 to 75 comprising determining a separation of the powered doors, and detecting the presence of an object in the doorway if the separation is below a predetermined distance. 77. A system substantially as described herein and/or as illustrated by accompanying Figures 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 or 7. 78. An apparatus substantially as described herein and/or as illustrated by accompanying Figures 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 or 7. 79. A method substantially as described herein and/or as illustrated by accompanying Figures 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 or 7.
